cityDNA.coreFantasy: A serpentine forest city where sacred groves, temple platforms, survey halls, guild craft, manor courts, and molting-mirror rites make transformation a civic principle.
cityDNA.readerTakeaway: Ssivareth is not a generic reptile jungle city; the uploaded images show a cultivated serpentfolk civic forest with sacred groves, public works, guilds, and elite garden courts.
cityDNA.civilizationThesis: Ssivareth turns serpent trance, ancestor guidance, root networks, temple processions, surveys, craft proof, and high residential power into a civilization of managed transformation.
cityDNA.visualIdentity: Limestone blocks, bamboo, obsidian, green marble, woven reed, sacred groves, hanging gardens, serpent-scale bands, temple platforms, survey halls, guildhalls, manor courts, and coiled-stone geometry.
cityDNA.culturalIdentity: Serpentine reptilian civic culture that values ritual renewal, controlled access, ancestor memory, forest stewardship, craft display, and elite hospitality.
cityDNA.symbolicIdentity: Scales, coils, molting mirrors, sacred groves, living canopy halls, procession stairs, blue lamps, woven banners, and salon pavilions symbolize renewal under law.

canonLocks: Ssivareth is a mature serpentine reptilian civic city, not a generic jungle backdrop.
The Wonder is Serpent Molting-Mirror Temple of Coiled Stone.
The Beacon is Sacred Grove Beacon.
The Seat of Power is Serpent Scale Bands Tide-Seal Citadel.
The five primary districts are Nature Preserve / Sacred Grove, Religious / Temple Quarter, Public Works Authority, Artisan Guilds, and High Residential.

Exact uploaded anchors include Nature Preserve / Sacred Grove Civic Hall, Sacred Grove Beacon, Hanging Gardens Monument, Living Canopy Hall Nook, Religious / Temple Quarter Civic Hall, Temple Platform, Religious Temple Quarter Lantern Monument, Procession Stair Nook, Public Works Authority Hall, Survey Hall, Blue-Lamp Monument, Permit Yard Nook, Artisan Guilds Civic Hall, Guildhall, Woven Banners Monument, Masterwork Arcade Nook, High Residential Civic Hall, Manor Court, Hanging Gardens Monument, and Salon Pavilion Nook.
provisionalCanon: Ssivareth's strongest civic read is coiled reverence shaped into institutions.
The uploaded image and sidecar pass were folded into v2 fields without adding a new top-level image-analysis section.
rejectedReads: pure temple city with no public works or guild life
cautionFlags: Preserve exact uploaded sidecar anchor names.
Keep grove, temple, survey, guild, and high-residential systems visible together.
Make serpent identity civic and ritual, not monster-coded scenery.

environment.environmentalLogic: The forest is structured civic space: sacred groves, survey yards, guild paths, and manor courts translate root networks into social order.

forms: Serpent Molting-Mirror Temple of Coiled Stone
skyline: Serpent Molting-Mirror Temple of Coiled Stone as far wonder anchor
Serpent Scale Bands Tide-Seal Citadel as central authority
Sacred Grove Beacon as forest signal landmark
temple platforms, survey halls, guild roofs, and manor courts under old growth canopy

civilization.people: Serpentine reptilian citizens are ritual-aware, observant, patient, and institutionally literate, moving through groves, halls, guild arcades, survey yards, and manor courts.
civilization.governance: Serpent Scale Bands Tide-Seal Citadel governs through grove rights, ritual status, public works permits, guild authority, high-house petitions, and ancestor guidance.
civilization.religion: Molting-mirror rites, sacred grove ceremonies, serpent trance, ancestor guidance, procession stairs, and temple platforms define renewal as a public obligation.

streets.appearance: Rooted civic paths move through sacred grove halls, temple platforms, survey offices, guild arcades, and manor gardens, with green marble, bamboo, reed screens, and blue lamps visible throughout.
streets.movement: Movement follows root paths, canopy halls, procession stairs, survey yards, guild corridors, and high residential garden routes.
streets.lighting: Green forest light and blue civic lamps divide sacred grove, temple, public works, guild, and manor spaces.
streets.weatherEffects: Rain brightens roots and stone, deepens grove color, and makes canopy routes and public drainage visible.
streets.dayCycle: Survey work, guild craft, grove tending, and manor errands dominate daytime.
streets.nightCycle: Ritual lanterns, temple processions, salon gatherings, and quiet grove watch dominate night.

housing.wealthy: High residential manor courts with hanging gardens, salon pavilions, green marble, bamboo screens, and controlled grove access.
housing.middle: Guild and public works homes along root paths with workrooms, reed screens, and shared gardens.
housing.poor: Compact root-edge dwellings near service paths and public yards, with shared lamps and practical woven materials.

districts.name: Nature Preserve / Sacred Grove
districts.civicPurpose: Sacred ecology, public signal, ancestral guidance, and ritual forest identity.
districts.visualIdentity: Nature Preserve / Sacred Grove Civic Hall, Sacred Grove Beacon, Hanging Gardens Monument, Living Canopy Hall Nook.
gameplayAssetNeeds: Nature Preserve / Sacred Grove Civic Hall

districts.name: Religious / Temple Quarter
districts.civicPurpose: Molting rites, processions, temple authority, and public renewal ceremonies.
districts.visualIdentity: Religious / Temple Quarter Civic Hall, Temple Platform, Religious Temple Quarter Lantern Monument, Procession Stair Nook.

districts.name: Public Works Authority
districts.civicPurpose: Surveying, permits, route maintenance, and forest-city growth control.
districts.visualIdentity: Public Works Authority Hall, Survey Hall, Blue-Lamp Monument, Permit Yard Nook.

districts.name: Artisan Guilds
districts.civicPurpose: Craft proof, guild legitimacy, masterwork display, and public production.
districts.visualIdentity: Artisan Guilds Civic Hall, Guildhall, Woven Banners Monument, Masterwork Arcade Nook.

districts.name: High Residential
districts.civicPurpose: Elite hospitality, manor influence, controlled garden access, and private-public status display.
districts.visualIdentity: High Residential Civic Hall, Manor Court, Hanging Gardens Monument, Salon Pavilion Nook.

wonder.name: Serpent Molting-Mirror Temple of Coiled Stone
wonder.symbolicRole: Renewal, ancestry, reflection, and coiled civic order made monumental.
wonder.visualRole: Far skyline temple of coiled stone, mirrored surfaces, green marble, and sacred forest approaches.
wonder.civicRole: Citywide rites of renewal, reflection, succession, and collective memory.
storyHooks: A molting-mirror rite reveals which old skin the city refuses to shed.

beacon.name: Sacred Grove Beacon
beacon.symbolicRole: Public warning rooted in sacred ecology.
beacon.visualRole: Beacon nested among sacred grove halls, hanging gardens, and living canopy paths.
beacon.civicRole: Coordinates grove response, temple movement, public works routes, guild notices, and manor warnings.
storyHooks: When the Beacon changes, the grove reacts before the court speaks.

seatOfPower.name: Serpent Scale Bands Tide-Seal Citadel
seatOfPower.symbolicRole: Serpent hierarchy, tide-seal authority, and civic memory banded into law.
seatOfPower.visualRole: Central citadel with scale-band ornament, green marble, sealed archives, and public terraces.
seatOfPower.civicRole: Governs grove rights, temple obligations, public works, guild claims, manor petitions, and renewal records.
storyHooks: A scale-band record shows who is allowed to renew and who must remain fixed.

majorLandmarks.name: Temple Platform
majorLandmarks.visualRole: Temple platform with lantern monument, procession stair, and coiled ceremonial geometry.
storyHooks: A procession stops at the stair that should have stayed empty.

majorLandmarks.name: Survey Hall
majorLandmarks.symbolicRole: Forest order measured rather than guessed.
majorLandmarks.visualRole: Survey hall with blue lamp, permit yard, maps, and root-network paths.
majorLandmarks.civicRole: Public works, permits, and route control.
storyHooks: A permit yard map reveals a root path older than the city’s law.

majorLandmarks.name: Guildhall
majorLandmarks.visualRole: Guildhall with woven banners, masterwork arcade, and forest-civic work courts.
majorLandmarks.civicRole: Guild authority, craft certification, and public making.
storyHooks: A masterwork exposes the shape of a hidden oath.

majorLandmarks.name: Manor Court
majorLandmarks.visualRole: Manor court with hanging gardens, salon pavilion, and controlled access paths.
majorLandmarks.civicRole: Elite hospitality, patronage, and high-house negotiation.
storyHooks: A salon invitation becomes more binding than a court order.

districtDioramaGuidance: S1 must show Nature Preserve / Sacred Grove Civic Hall, Sacred Grove Beacon, Hanging Gardens Monument, and Living Canopy Hall Nook.
S2 must show Religious / Temple Quarter Civic Hall, Temple Platform, Religious Temple Quarter Lantern Monument, and Procession Stair Nook.
S3 must show Public Works Authority Hall, Survey Hall, Blue-Lamp Monument, and Permit Yard Nook.
S4 must show Artisan Guilds Civic Hall, Guildhall, Woven Banners Monument, and Masterwork Arcade Nook.
S5 must show High Residential Civic Hall, Manor Court, Hanging Gardens Monument, and Salon Pavilion Nook.
mapBackgroundGuidance: Keep Serpent Molting-Mirror Temple of Coiled Stone, Sacred Grove Beacon, and Serpent Scale Bands Tide-Seal Citadel legible together.
Use limestone, bamboo, obsidian, green marble, woven reed, sacred groves, temple platforms, survey halls, guildhalls, manor courts, and coiled-stone geometry.
requiredVisualElements: Serpent Molting-Mirror Temple of Coiled Stone
Nature Preserve / Sacred Grove Civic Hall
